articles.title: Reduce GVisor Cold Starts with GPU Snapshotting
articles.summary: Reducing GPU Cold Starts with Memory Snapshots: Restoring CUDA Workloads in Seconds Cerebrium has been obsessed with the cold start problem since day one, pushing to rethink almost every layer of its infrastructure. The application starts accumulating complexity around one problem: getting a model ready to serve traffic fast enough. articles.title: How We Made IPFS Content Publishing 10x Faster
articles.summary: Publishing content in Distributed Hash Tables has traditionally been a slow operation, especially when large networks or nodes are involved. The ProbeLab team identified this problem years ago through extensive measurements and proposed an optimisation that improves performance by reducing the content publication time to over one order of magnitude while simultaneously reducing network overhead by 40%. This technique was recently released as Optimistic Provide in IPFS's Kubo 0.39.0, with results showing a significant decrease in upload latency from more than 13 seconds to less than 1 second.

articles.title: US Supreme Court just blew up EU-US Data Transfers
articles.summary: The US Supreme Court has ruled that the US Federal Trade Commission ("FTC") may not be independent anymore due to changes in US law and regulations. This decision affects EU-US data transfers where the European Commission relies on the FTC as the enforcer of EU-US deals on personal data. The ruling means the EU can no longer rely solely on the FTC for oversight, which is a requirement under EU treaty law and Article 8(3) of the Charter of Fundamental Rights. This change may impact companies that outsource processing of personal data to US cloud providers.

articles.title: Internal Combustion Engine
articles.summary: The internal combustion engine's basic components, including the handle, crank arm, and shaft, work together to generate rotational motion through a force applied at a distance from the shaft, resulting in torque that converts linear force into mechanical energy. This principle is also used in manual coffee grinders and bicycles, where a similar cranking mechanism is employed to power movement without relying on human labor. A cannon's explosive power can be harnessed to generate significant rotational motion, but its effectiveness is limited by the need for a reliable source of force and direction.

articles.title: Box3D, an open source 3D physics engine
articles.summary: Box3D is an open source 3D physics engine that extends the capabilities of Box2D with features such as triangle mesh collision and height-field collision, allowing for more realistic simulations in games. The core architecture remains similar to Box2D, but with additional functionality including sub-stepping solver, continuous collision, graph coloring, and large world support. This new engine is designed to be used by developers who need a robust physics solution for their 3D games, particularly those that require managing hundreds of thousands of entities on the server.

articles.title: Building a passive Ethernet tap

articles.title: Fixing a kubelet memory leak in Kubernetes 1.36
articles.summary: A kubelet process in a Kubernetes cluster has grown to an unexpected size, causing memory pressure that led to the restart of Pods on the node but failed to show up when using kubectl top pods. The application running on the node was not experiencing memory growth and did not reach its memory limits, suggesting the leak is related to the kubelet process itself rather than the applications running on it. Dumping the kubelet heap profile using Go's pprof package revealed a significant increase in memory usage, which can be used to identify the source of the leak.
